raw.objective: “to look for a set of PK parameters of CBZ that might help optimization of drug dosage among Egyptian epileptic patients.”
raw.covariates: PK parameters were taken from literature, no new relations were estimated.
raw.observations: A large improvement in predictable PK was found after inclusion of only 1 concentration value per patient, showing the impact of TDM in optimising dosing strategy. It was also found that values from literature could easily be applied to new datasets instead of building a new model.
raw.equations: Different sets of parameter values, see paper

extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to look for a set of PK parameters of CBZ that might help optimization of drug dosage among Egyptian epileptic patients.”

tested: PK parameters were taken from literature
covariates.raw: PK parameters were taken from literature, no new relations were estimated.

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
